implementedBehavior.normalChatSend: unchanged for non-prefixed messages; event handlers now delegate click/Enter through window.handleSendButtonAction, which then falls through to chatManager.handleSendMessage when no Codex Router command is present
implementedBehavior.governedPath: prefixed input builds a task-envelope.v1 object and calls window.codexRouterHostClient.run(task)
implementedBehavior.fallback: if CodexRouterHost is unavailable or the command has no task body, the governed command is consumed and reports a UI toast instead of silently falling back to unmanaged chat
implementedBehavior.buttonState: send button disabled state is restored to its previous value after the governed command completes

notValidated: fresh live Electron renderer command-path smoke
manual UI test of /codex-router or /codex-task input
acceptanceInstruction: Restart or reload VCPChat before manual UI acceptance because renderer.js and modules/event-listeners.js are loaded by the Electron renderer.
nextGate: After VCPChat restart, enter a safe prefixed command such as /codex-router inspect current router status, then confirm that the command path uses CodexRouterHost and that ordinary chat still sends normally.
